meta_title: Impact Windows in Laud Lakes, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Protect your Laud Lakes home with hurricane-rated impact windows. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs ESW & CWI windows with 170mph wind ratings. Free quotes, financing from $87/mo.
h1: Impact Windows in Laud Lakes, FL — Fortify Your Home Against South Florida Storms
intro:
Laud Lakes homeowners know that living in Broward County means living with hurricane season as a constant reality. Situated about 8 miles inland from the Atlantic coast, the Laud Lakes area — including the Lauderdale Lakes North community — sits squarely in a 170mph wind zone, which means the structures here are expected to withstand the kind of punishing wind loads that only properly rated, impact-resistant windows can reliably handle. If your home still has single-pane glass or older aluminum windows, you're not just rolling the dice on storm damage — you're leaving real money on the table every single month through energy loss and unnecessary insurance premiums.

With roughly 55% of homes in the area built before 1994, the window of vulnerability is wide open — literally. Prior to the modern Florida Building Code revisions that followed Hurricane Andrew, residential construction standards were dramatically weaker than what's required today. That means a significant portion of the homes near Lake Side Park and throughout the surrounding streets were built with windows never designed to meet today's wind or impact standards. Upgrading to modern impact windows isn't just a smart move — for many Laud Lakes homeowners, it's an overdue one. Average home values in the area sit around $270,000, and protecting that investment with code-compliant, impact-rated windows is one of the highest-return upgrades a homeowner can make.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've made it our mission to serve South Florida communities like Laud Lakes with honest, high-quality installations that stand behind every product we put in your home. We understand the specific demands of Broward County's building environment, the permit process through the Broward County Building Division, and the real-world wind exposure that homes in this part of the county face. Whether you're protecting a family home, managing an investment property, or simply looking to reduce your energy bills and lower your insurance premiums, our team brings the expertise and products to get the job done right — every time.

section_why.h2: Why Laud Lakes Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
When you invite a contractor into your home to replace windows, you're trusting them with one of your most important assets. That's why Premier Impact Windows & Roofing operates on a 100% in-house installation model — no subcontractors, no third-party crews, no strangers being shuffled through a revolving door. Every technician who shows up at a Laud Lakes home is a trained, vetted member of our own team, backed by over 60 years of combined installation experience across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. That's not just a talking point — it's the foundation of why we've earned an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau and completed more than 500 homes throughout South Florida.

Neighborhoods like Lauderdale Lakes North present their own unique considerations. Many homes here feature older construction with non-standard rough openings, aging frames, and architectural details that require precise measurement and custom-fit solutions. Our crews are experienced working with pre-1994 construction, where window replacement isn't always a straightforward swap. We assess each opening carefully, prepare the substrate properly, and install every unit to manufacturer specs and Florida Building Code requirements — because anything less puts your home and your permit at risk.

As a company, we carry full licensing and insurance, and we handle the entire permitting process through the Broward County Building Division on your behalf. You don't need to navigate bureaucracy, make phone calls, or worry about inspection scheduling. We manage that process from start to finish, so your installation is not only done well — it's done legally and with full documentation. That documentation matters enormously when it comes time to file for insurance credits or sell your home.

We also believe that quality hurricane protection shouldn't be out of reach for working families in communities like Laud Lakes. That's why we offer flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down programs available for qualified homeowners. The Kings of Service aren't just a tagline — it's the standard we hold ourselves to on every single project.

section_history.h2: A Storm History That Demands Serious Protection in Laud Lakes
section_history.body:
The Laud Lakes area has not been spared by South Florida's most destructive weather events. Since 1990, the region has experienced 9 notable storm systems, with three standing out as true benchmarks of destruction. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was a category 5 catastrophe that reshaped building codes across Florida and exposed just how catastrophically unprepared many homes were for major hurricane force winds. Though Andrew's direct impact was centered further south in Miami-Dade, its outer bands still affected Broward County communities significantly, and its legacy was a complete overhaul of Florida's residential construction standards — standards that the majority of Laud Lakes homes predate.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 hit Broward County head-on as a category 3 storm and caused widespread structural damage, roof failures, and window blow-outs across communities like Laud Lakes, its neighboring city of Tamarac, and North Lauderdale. Homeowners with older single-pane windows experienced glass failures that led to interior wind pressure buildup — one of the primary causes of roof loss during hurricanes. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which traversed much of South Florida and reminded an entire generation of newer homeowners just how fast conditions can deteriorate. Flooding, downed trees, and wind-driven debris caused millions in losses across Broward County.

The pattern is consistent and instructive: every major storm cycle reveals the same weak points — windows and doors. Impact-resistant windows with laminated glass don't just resist wind pressure; they hold together even when struck by debris, preventing the dangerous interior pressure changes that can lift roofs and collapse walls. For Laud Lakes homeowners, the storm history isn't a scare tactic — it's a curriculum. The lesson is clear.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Windows in Broward County
section_code.body:
Laud Lakes falls within Broward County's jurisdiction, which means all window replacements must comply with the Florida Building Code (FBC) and be permitted through the Broward County Building Division. While the Laud Lakes area is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — that designation is reserved for Miami-Dade and Broward coastal zones — the area is still subject to strict wind-resistance requirements given its 170mph design wind speed classification. That means any window installed in a Laud Lakes home must carry a current Florida Product Approval (FPA) number and be rated for the applicable design pressure loads specific to the home's location, height, and exposure category.

Product approval isn't just paperwork — it's a guarantee that the window has been independently tested and certified to perform under specific wind and impact conditions. The FBC requires that all opening protections in a 170mph wind zone meet minimum design pressure ratings, meaning not just any impact window qualifies. The window must match the calculated DP rating for your home's specific geometry. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's installation team performs accurate load calculations and selects the appropriate certified products for every project, ensuring your installation passes inspection the first time.

Permitting in Broward County requires submission of product approval documents, installation drawings, and in some cases structural engineering data depending on the scope of the project. After installation, a county inspector visits the home to verify that the installation meets code. We manage this entire process — from permit application to final inspection sign-off — on behalf of every Laud Lakes homeowner we work with. The result is a compliant, documented installation that protects your home, your insurance policy, and the resale value of your property.

section_products.h2: ESW Eastern Storm Windows and CWI — Premium Impact Window Solutions Built for Broward County
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading product lines in Laud Lakes homes: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are designed and engineered specifically for Florida's extreme weather environment, and both carry Florida Product Approvals required for installation in Broward County's 170mph wind zone. These aren't off-the-shelf products adapted for hurricane use — they're purpose-built systems developed with South Florida's unique climate demands in mind.

ESW impact windows feature heavy-duty multi-chamber aluminum frames engineered to resist deflection under extreme wind loads, paired with laminated safety glass that holds together on impact rather than shattering into dangerous shards. The laminated glass construction — typically a PVB or SGP interlayer bonded between two glass lites — is what gives impact windows their defining characteristic: even when cracked by hurricane debris, the glass stays in place, maintaining the building envelope and preventing catastrophic interior pressure changes. ESW products also incorporate Low-E coatings that reflect infrared radiation, dramatically reducing solar heat gain and helping Laud Lakes homeowners cut cooling costs year-round.

CWI products offer equally impressive performance with a focus on versatility and aesthetic flexibility, making them a strong choice for older Laud Lakes homes where matching existing architectural character matters. CWI's multi-chamber frame design provides superior thermal performance and structural rigidity, and their product line covers everything from single-hung and double-hung configurations to sliding windows, casements, and picture windows. Both ESW and CWI products meet or exceed the design pressure requirements for Broward County installations, and both come backed by manufacturer warranties that complement Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's own workmanship guarantee.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Windows Cost in Laud Lakes — And Why the Investment Pays Off
section_cost.body:
For a typical Laud Lakes home valued around $270,000, a full impact window replacement project generally ranges from $8,000 to $20,000 depending on the number of openings, window sizes, configurations, and selected product lines. Homes with more complex layouts, multiple stories, or custom-sized openings will fall toward the higher end of that range. Single-story homes with standard-sized openings and a moderate number of windows are often completed well within that range. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides detailed, transparent quotes with no hidden fees, so you know exactly what you're paying for before a single window is ordered.

To make this essential home improvement accessible for Laud Lakes families, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month through our lending partners, with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. Spreading the cost over time means you don't have to choose between protecting your family and managing your monthly budget. And because impact windows begin saving you money from the moment they're installed — through reduced energy bills, eliminated storm shutter costs, and lower insurance premiums — many homeowners find the real out-of-pocket cost is far less than the sticker price suggests.

Consider the return on investment more broadly: eliminating the need to purchase, store, and install hurricane shutters each season saves hundreds of dollars annually and hours of labor. Energy savings from Low-E coated impact glass can reduce cooling costs by 15–30% in a South Florida home. And the insurance premium reductions that come with a documented wind mitigation inspection can shave hundreds of dollars per year off your homeowner's policy. Over a 10-year horizon, the financial case for impact windows in Laud Lakes is compelling — and the peace of mind they provide is priceless.

section_insurance.h2: Unlock Major Insurance Savings with Impact Windows in Broward County
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediate and measurable financial benefits of installing impact windows in Laud Lakes is the reduction in homeowner's insurance premiums. Florida's insurance market is notoriously expensive — particularly in Broward County, where carriers price policies based heavily on wind exposure and opening protection. When you upgrade to code-compliant, impact-rated windows, you become eligible for significant wind mitigation credits that can reduce the windstorm portion of your premium by a meaningful margin. For many Laud Lakes homeowners, those savings range from 15% to 45% depending on the carrier and the scope of the upgrades.

The process begins with a wind mitigation inspection conducted by a licensed inspector after your impact windows are installed and permitted. The inspector documents the type of opening protection on your home and submits a standardized OIR-B1-1802 Wind Mitigation Report to your insurance carrier. That report triggers a re-rating of your policy, and the credits are applied at your next renewal — or sometimes sooner. Common carriers operating in Broward County, including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, and others, all recognize impact window installations as a significant risk-reduction factor.

Beyond the annual premium savings, impact windows can also stabilize your insurability. In today's South Florida insurance environment, where carriers are dropping policies and restricting coverage, having fully protected openings makes your home a more attractive risk to insure. Laud Lakes homeowners who've been dropped by a carrier or are struggling with high-risk surcharges often find that a documented impact window installation changes their standing. It's not just about saving money — it's about maintaining access to affordable coverage in a market that's becoming increasingly difficult to navigate.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact windows if Laud Lakes isn't directly on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Laud Lakes is located in a 170mph wind zone, meaning your home faces serious wind load exposure during major hurricane events regardless of its distance from the shoreline. Wind-driven debris, sustained high winds, and pressure differentials during a storm don't discriminate by distance from the coast. Hurricane Wilma and Irma caused significant inland damage throughout Broward County. Impact windows protect your home from all of these threats and are required under the Florida Building Code for new and replacement window installations in this area.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost in Laud Lakes, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: For most Laud Lakes homes, a full impact window project runs between $8,000 and $20,000 depending on the number of openings, configurations, and product selections. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. We also provide free, no-obligation quotes so you know exactly what to expect before committing. Combined with energy savings and insurance discounts, the net cost over time is significantly less than the upfront investment.

faqs.q: How does the permit process work for impact windows in Broward County?
faqs.a: All impact window replacements in Laud Lakes must be permitted through the Broward County Building Division. The permit requires Florida Product Approval documentation for the windows being installed, installation drawings, and applicable load calculations. After installation, a county inspector visits the property for a final inspection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages this entire process on your behalf — from initial permit application through final inspection sign-off — so you don't have to coordinate with the county or worry about compliance.

faqs.q: How long does an impact window installation take for a typical Laud Lakes home?
faqs.a: For most single-family homes in Laud Lakes, the physical installation of impact windows takes one to three days depending on the number of openings and complexity of the project. The overall timeline from contract signing to project completion typically runs three to six weeks once you factor in permit approval, product ordering, and scheduling. We'll walk you through the full timeline at your consultation so you can plan accordingly. Our in-house crew works efficiently without cutting corners, ensuring a clean, precise installation every time.

faqs.q: Will impact windows actually lower my homeowner's insurance in Laud Lakes?
faqs.a: Yes — in most cases, significantly. After your impact windows are installed and permitted, you can request a wind mitigation inspection. The resulting OIR-B1-1802 report documents your home's opening protection and is submitted to your insurance carrier. Broward County homeowners commonly see windstorm premium reductions of 15% to 45% depending on their carrier and overall risk profile. These are recurring annual savings that continue year after year, making impact windows one of the highest-ROI improvements a Laud Lakes homeowner can make.
